title: Decision Flow for Applying the Rule of Sum vs. Rule of Product
description: A flowchart to help determine whether to use the Rule of Sum (for mutually exclusive choices) or the Rule of Product (for sequential choices) when counting possibilities.
elements: Start: Count the number of ways to perform a task
Are the choices made in sequence (one after another)?
Are the choices made from separate, non-overlapping sets?
Rule of Product: Multiply the number of choices at each step
Rule of Sum: Add the number of choices from each set
Result: Total number of distinct outcomes
relationships: Start leads to decision 'Are the choices made in sequence?'
'Yes' from sequence decision leads to 'Rule of Product'
'No' from sequence decision leads to decision 'Are choices from separate sets?'
'Yes' from separate sets decision leads to 'Rule of Sum'
'No' from separate sets decision leads back to start for re-evaluation
Both Rule of Sum and Rule of Product lead to the final Result
